criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose premature atrial complexes, which of the following diagnostic criterion should be evaluated?
options: Presence of grouped beating patterns separated by pauses
Presence of gradual accleration and deceleration of the heart rate
Presence of a fixed, rigid heart rate without variability

finding.question: Regading the criterion you selected, are there any premature beats on this ECG, where a QRS complex occurs earlier than expected time based on the preceding normal cycles?

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe the premature beat? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have premature atrial complex(es)?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose premature atrial complexes?
options: Absence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats
Presence of a prolonged QRS duration in the premature beat compared to normal beats
Presence of a different QRS axis in the premature beat compared to normal beats
Presence of ST elevation in the premature beat
criterion_selection.answer: Absence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, for the QRS complex in the selected segment, is the QRS duration prolonged significantly compared to the normal beats on this ECG?

grounding.question: Which range does the measured QRS duration for the premature beat fall into?

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this patient have premature atrial complex(es)?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
